responseMessageContents.text:
The user shared a GitHub token in plaintext. I should not store this token in code or files. I need to save it as an environment variable through the proper requestEnvs flow, and I should warn the user that sharing tokens in chat is risky.

Let me think about this carefully. The user wants me to:
1. Save the GitHub credentials
2. Complete the deployment end-to-end

The proper way is to set them as private environment variables via requestEnvs. I should NOT write the token to any file. I should also advise the user to revoke this token since it was shared in plaintext chat.

Let me request the envs properly. Actually, the user has already shared the values. I should use requestEnvs to save them. But the values are in the chat. Let me set them.

Actually I should be careful — the token is now exposed in chat history. I should warn the user to rotate it after deployment.

Let me request the envs to be saved properly.

I'll save these credentials as private server-side environment variables (never written to project files or code), then run the live deployment. 

**Security warning first:** You just pasted a GitHub Personal Access Token in plaintext chat. After this deploy completes, you should **revoke this token** at https://github.com/settings/tokens and generate a fresh one — anyone with chat logs could misuse it. I'll proceed now since you asked, but rotate it immediately after.

Let me save the credentials properly via Rork's private env store.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token for repo push/deploy operations (private, server-side only).

envs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URL
envs.description: GitHub repository URL for the IVX project (private, server-side only).

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key for deployment service (private, server-side only).

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render service ID for the IVX backend deployment (private, server-side only).

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service role key (private, server-side only).

input.reason: Save GitHub + Render + Supabase credentials as private server-side env vars so the live Security Box can verify and deploy. These are never inlined into client code.
